---
title: "AnalysisMM"
author: "jjdomg"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(writexl)
library(knitr)
```

## Calling the read_base

`
```{r reading_files}
workflow_data <- readRDS("workflow_data.rds")

# Loading cb_filter
cb_filter <- workflow_data$cb_filter
```

## Preparing the base

```{r base_MM_analysis}
base_MM <- cb_filter |> 
        filter(Dx=="MM") |>
        select(Id, data, )

#rm(aneuploidies, cb_filter, complete_base, demograph, metadata, quality, variants)

filter_MM <-read_tsv("filter_MM.bed", 
                     skip = 1, 
                     col_names = c("chrom", "start", "end", "name"),
                     col_types = "iiic")

filter_MM <- filter_MM %>%
  mutate(type = case_when(
    str_detect(name, fixed("insertion", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ 1,
    str_detect(name, fixed("deletion", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ 2,
    str_detect(name, fixed("inversion", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ 3,
    str_detect(name, fixed("translocation", ignore_case = TRUE)) | 
    str_detect(name, fixed("rearrangement", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ 4,
    str_detect(name, fixed("duplication", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ 5,
    str_detect(name, fixed("gain", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ 6,
    TRUE ~ 0  # Si no encuentra nada de lo anterior
  ))

```

## Adjusting padding

```{r padding}
cnv_overlap <- 600000 #Just to include Id=107 rMAF 
sv_overlap <- 0
```



## Creating the MATRIX

```{r matrix_generation}
library(tidyverse)

check_variant <- function(sample_data, f_chrom, f_start, f_end, f_type, f_name) {
  
  f_start_adj <- f_start - cnv_overlap
  f_end_adj <- f_end + cnv_overlap
  
  igh_chrom <- 14
  igh_start <- 105583731 - cnv_overlap
  igh_end <- 106879812 + cnv_overlap
  
  # Filtrado inicial
  if (f_type == 4) {
    hits <- sample_data %>%
      filter(as.character(RefcontigID1) == as.character(f_chrom) | 
             as.character(RefcontigID2) == as.character(f_chrom))
  } else {
    hits <- sample_data %>%
      filter(as.character(RefcontigID1) == as.character(f_chrom))
  }
  
  if (nrow(hits) == 0) return(0)
  
  # Aplicamos la lógica de solapamiento
  final_hits <- hits %>%
    filter(
      case_when(
        f_type == 0 ~ (RefStartPos <= f_end_adj & RefEndPos >= f_start_adj),
        f_type == 2 ~ (SuperType %in% 2 | tolower(as.character(Type)) == "loss") & 
                      (RefStartPos <= f_end_adj & RefEndPos >= f_start_adj),
        f_type == 4 ~ (SuperType %in% 4) & (
          (as.character(RefcontigID1) == as.character(f_chrom) & 
           RefStartPos >= f_start_adj & RefStartPos <= f_end_adj &
           (!str_detect(f_name, "IGH_translocation") | 
            (as.character(RefcontigID2) == as.character(igh_chrom) & 
             RefEndPos >= igh_start & RefEndPos <= igh_end))) | 
          (as.character(RefcontigID2) == as.character(f_chrom) & 
           RefEndPos >= f_start_adj & RefEndPos <= f_end_adj &
           (!str_detect(f_name, "IGH_translocation") | 
            (as.character(RefcontigID1) == as.character(igh_chrom) & 
             RefStartPos >= igh_start & RefStartPos <= igh_end)))
        ),
        f_type == 6 ~ (tolower(as.character(Type)) == "gain") & 
                      (RefStartPos <= f_end_adj & RefEndPos >= f_start_adj),
        TRUE ~ (SuperType %in% f_type) & (RefStartPos <= f_end_adj & RefEndPos >= f_start_adj)
      )
    )
  
  if(nrow(final_hits) == 0) return(0)
  
  # NUEVA LÓGICA: Si el tipo es 0, devolvemos el código de la alteración encontrada
  if(f_type == 0) {
    # Tomamos la primera alteración encontrada en esa región
    tipo_alt <- tolower(as.character(final_hits$Type[1]))
    
    res <- case_when(
      tipo_alt == "deletion" ~ 2,
      tipo_alt %in% c("duplication", "duplication_split") ~ 3,
      tipo_alt %in% c("translocation_interchr", "translocation_intrachr") ~ 4,
      tipo_alt == "insertion" ~ 5,
      tipo_alt == "gain" ~ 6,
      tipo_alt == "loss" ~ 7,
      TRUE ~ 1 # Valor por defecto si hay solapamiento pero no coincide el nombre exacto
    )
    return(res)
  }
  
  # Si no es tipo 0, devolvemos 1 (binario) como antes
  return(1)
}

complete_matrix <- base_MM %>%
  mutate(presence = map(data, function(sample_df) {
    
    results_vector <- pmap_dbl(filter_MM, function(chrom, start, end, name, type) {
      check_variant(sample_df, chrom, start, end, type, name)
    })
    
    as_tibble(as.list(setNames(results_vector, filter_MM$name)), .name_repair = "minimal")
    
  })) %>%
  select(Id, presence) %>%
  unnest(presence)

complete_matrix <- as.data.frame(complete_matrix)

# A partir de aquí puedes seguir con el código de "Adding aneuploidies" 
# usando complete_matrix en lugar de final_matrix.


# 2. Procesamiento principal sin warnings
final_matrix <- base_MM %>%
  mutate(presence = map(data, function(sample_df) {
    
    # Calculamos los resultados
    results_vector <- pmap_dbl(filter_MM, function(chrom, start, end, name, type) {
      check_variant(sample_df, chrom, start, end, type, name)
    })
    
    # Creamos el tibble directamente con nombres para evitar el paso de matriz sin nombre
    # Usamos .name_repair = "minimal" para que no se queje si hay nombres repetidos
    as_tibble(as.list(setNames(results_vector, filter_MM$name)), .name_repair = "minimal")
    
  })) %>%
  select(Id, presence) %>%
  unnest(presence)

final_matrix <- as.data.frame(final_matrix)


write_xlsx(final_matrix, "bases/final_matrix.xlsx")
```

## Adding aneuploidies

```{r aneuploidy_generation}
# 1. Creamos el vector de nombres con 1:22, X e Y
chrom_list <- c(as.character(1:22), "X", "Y")
chrom_names <- map(chrom_list, ~c(paste0("Loss_", .x), paste0("Gain_", .x))) %>% unlist()

# 2. Extraemos la información de aneuploidías de base_MM con lógica de niveles
aneuploidy_data <- base_MM %>%
  mutate(aneu_cols = map(data, function(df) {
    # Inicializamos vector de ceros (ahora contendrá los niveles 1, 2, 3...)
    vec <- setNames(rep(0, length(chrom_names)), chrom_names)
    
    # Filtramos y calculamos el "valor" según fractionalCopyNumber
    anes <- df %>% 
      filter(Type %in% c("an-loss", "an-gain")) %>%
      mutate(
        chrom_label = case_when(
          RefcontigID1 == 23 ~ "X",
          RefcontigID1 == 24 ~ "Y",
          TRUE ~ as.character(RefcontigID1)
        ),
        # Lógica de asignación de niveles según tu escala
        valor_aneu = case_when(
          Type == "an-loss" & fractionalCopyNumber >= 0.50 & fractionalCopyNumber <= 1.99 ~ 1,
          Type == "an-loss" & fractionalCopyNumber >= 0.00 & fractionalCopyNumber <= 0.50 ~ 2,
          
          Type == "an-gain" & fractionalCopyNumber >= 3.00 & fractionalCopyNumber <= 3.99 ~ 1,
          Type == "an-gain" & fractionalCopyNumber >= 4.00 & fractionalCopyNumber <= 4.99 ~ 2,
          Type == "an-gain" & fractionalCopyNumber >= 5.00 & fractionalCopyNumber <= 5.99 ~ 3,
          Type == "an-gain" & fractionalCopyNumber >= 6.00 & fractionalCopyNumber <= 6.99 ~ 4,
          Type == "an-gain" & fractionalCopyNumber >= 7.00 & fractionalCopyNumber <= 7.99 ~ 5,
          TRUE ~ 0
        ),
        col_name = case_when(
          Type == "an-loss" ~ paste0("Loss_", chrom_label),
          Type == "an-gain" ~ paste0("Gain_", chrom_label)
        )
      ) %>%
      filter(valor_aneu > 0) # Solo nos quedamos con las que entran en la escala
    
    # Marcamos con el valor correspondiente en lugar de un 1 fijo
    if(nrow(anes) > 0) {
      # Usamos un bucle simple o asignación indexada para poblar el vector
      for(i in 1:nrow(anes)) {
        nombre_col <- anes$col_name[i]
        if(nombre_col %in% chrom_names) {
          vec[nombre_col] <- anes$valor_aneu[i]
        }
      }
    }
    
    return(as_tibble(as.list(vec)))
  })) %>%
  select(Id, aneu_cols) %>%
  unnest(aneu_cols)

# 3. Unimos a tu matriz final
final_matrix_extended <- final_matrix %>%
  left_join(aneuploidy_data, by = "Id")

## CONTANDO CROMOSOMAS

#Identificar cuáles son las columnas de Ganancia y cuáles las de Pérdida
cols_gain <- grep("^Gain_", names(final_matrix_extended), value = TRUE)
cols_loss <- grep("^Loss_", names(final_matrix_extended), value = TRUE)

#Calcular la columna TotalChr_Stimated
# La lógica es: 46 (basal) + Suma de dosis de ganancias - Suma de dosis de pérdidas
final_matrix_extended <- final_matrix_extended %>%
  mutate(TotalChr_Stimated = 46 + 
           rowSums(select(., all_of(cols_gain)), na.rm = TRUE) - 
           rowSums(select(., all_of(cols_loss)), na.rm = TRUE))

#Ploidía
final_matrix_extended <- final_matrix_extended %>%
  mutate(Ploidy = case_when(
    TotalChr_Stimated >= 24 & TotalChr_Stimated <= 34 ~ 1,
    TotalChr_Stimated >= 35 & TotalChr_Stimated <= 45 ~ 2,
    TotalChr_Stimated == 46                          ~ 3,
    TotalChr_Stimated >= 47                          ~ 4,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_  # Para valores fuera de rango
  )) %>%
  # Convertimos a factor: los niveles son los números, las etiquetas son los nombres
  mutate(Ploidy = factor(Ploidy, 
                         levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
                         labels = c("Hiperhaploide", 
                                    "No hiperdiploide", 
                                    "Diploide", 
                                    "Hiperdiploide")))



# 4. Exportar
write_xlsx(final_matrix_extended, "bases/final_matrix_completa.xlsx")
```

## OncoPrint

```{r oncoprint}
# Instalación (si no los tienes)
# if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
# BiocManager::install("ComplexHeatmap")
# install.packages("readxl")

library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(readxl)

# 1. Cargar los datos
datos <- final_matrix_extended

# 2. Convertir a matriz y trasponer
# Excluimos 'Id' (col 1) y las últimas columnas clínicas (Ploidy, etc.) si no son alteraciones
# Basado en tu archivo, las alteraciones van de la columna 2 a la 76
matriz_ogm <- as.matrix(datos[, 2:76])
rownames(matriz_ogm) <- datos$Id
matriz_ogm <- t(matriz_ogm) # Transponemos: Filas = Alteraciones, Columnas = Pacientes

# 3. Reemplazar 1 por el nombre de la alteración (ej. "MUT") y 0 por vacío
# Esto es necesario porque oncoPrint busca etiquetas de texto
matriz_final <- apply(matriz_ogm, 2, function(x) ifelse(x == 1, "ALT", ""))

# Definir el color de la alteración
col = c("ALT" = "#E41A1C") # Rojo para las alteraciones

# Definir la función de dibujo (un rectángulo que llena el hueco)
alter_fun = list(
    background = function(x, y, w, h) {
        grid.rect(x, y, w-unit(0.5, "mm"), h-unit(0.5, "mm"), 
            gp = gpar(fill = "#CCCCCC", col = NA))
    },
    ALT = function(x, y, w, h) {
        grid.rect(x, y, w-unit(0.5, "mm"), h-unit(0.5, "mm"), 
            gp = gpar(fill = col["ALT"], col = NA))
    }
)

# Generar el gráfico
oncoPrint(matriz_final,
          alter_fun = alter_fun, 
          col = col, 
          column_title = "OncoPrint de Alteraciones OGM",
          heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Alteración", at = c("ALT"), 
                                      labels = c("Detectada")),
          remove_empty_columns = TRUE, # Quita pacientes sin ninguna alteración
          remove_empty_rows = TRUE)    # Quita alteraciones no presentes en ningún paciente

# Crear anotación inferior con la columna 'Ploidy'
# 1. Extraer los niveles únicos de Ploidía para no olvidar ninguno
niveles_ploidia <- unique(datos$Ploidy)

# 2. Crear una paleta de colores (puedes cambiarlos a tu gusto)
# Usamos una función para generar colores si hay muchos niveles
colores_ploidia <- setNames(
  c("blue", "orange", "green", "purple", "red")[1:length(niveles_ploidia)], 
  niveles_ploidia
)

# 3. Crear la anotación corregida
anno_inferior = HeatmapAnnotation(
    Ploidia = datos$Ploidy,
    Total_Chr = anno_barplot(datos$TotalChr_Stimated),
    col = list(Ploidia = colores_ploidia), # Ahora incluye todos los niveles
    show_annotation_name = TRUE
)
```

```{r oncoprint2}
# Transformar los números en etiquetas de texto para ComplexHeatmap
complete_matrix <- base_MM %>%
  mutate(presence = map(data, function(sample_df) {
    results_vector <- pmap_dbl(filter_MM, function(chrom, start, end, name, type) {
      check_variant(sample_df, chrom, start, end, type, name)
    })
    as_tibble(as.list(setNames(results_vector, filter_MM$name)), .name_repair = "minimal")
  })) %>%
  select(Id, presence) %>%
  unnest(presence)

complete_matrix <- as.data.frame(complete_matrix)

# --- 5. PREPARACIÓN PARA ONCOPRINT ---
# Transformamos la matriz numérica a una de caracteres (etiquetas)
matriz_plot <- apply(as.matrix(complete_matrix[, 2:ncol(complete_matrix)]), 2, function(x) {
  case_when(
    x == 1 ~ "ALT",
    x == 2 ~ "DEL",
    x == 3 ~ "DUP",
    x == 4 ~ "TRA",
    x == 5 ~ "INS",
    x == 6 ~ "GAIN",
    x == 7 ~ "LOSS",
    TRUE ~ ""
  )
})
rownames(matriz_plot) <- complete_matrix$Id
matriz_plot <- t(matriz_plot)

# Definición de Colores
col = c("ALT"  = "#E41A1C", # Rojo
        "DEL"  = "#377EB8", # Azul
        "DUP"  = "#4DAF4A", # Verde
        "TRA"  = "#984EA3", # Morado
        "INS"  = "#FF7F00", # Naranja
        "GAIN" = "#A65628", # Marrón
        "LOSS" = "#F781BF") # Rosa

# Función de dibujo (rectángulos)
alter_fun = list(
  background = function(x, y, w, h) {
    grid.rect(x, y, w-unit(0.5, "mm"), h-unit(0.5, "mm"), 
              gp = gpar(fill = "#CCCCCC", col = NA))
  },
  ALT  = function(x, y, w, h) { grid.rect(x, y, w-unit(0.5, "mm"), h-unit(0.5, "mm"), gp = gpar(fill = col["ALT"], col = NA)) },
  DEL  = function(x, y, w, h) { grid.rect(x, y, w-unit(0.5, "mm"), h-unit(0.5, "mm"), gp = gpar(fill = col["DEL"], col = NA)) },
  DUP  = function(x, y, w, h) { grid.rect(x, y, w-unit(0.5, "mm"), h-unit(0.5, "mm"), gp = gpar(fill = col["DUP"], col = NA)) },
  TRA  = function(x, y, w, h) { grid.rect(x, y, w-unit(0.5, "mm"), h-unit(0.5, "mm"), gp = gpar(fill = col["TRA"], col = NA)) },
  INS  = function(x, y, w, h) { grid.rect(x, y, w-unit(0.5, "mm"), h-unit(0.5, "mm"), gp = gpar(fill = col["INS"], col = NA)) },
  GAIN = function(x, y, w, h) { grid.rect(x, y, w-unit(0.5, "mm"), h-unit(0.5, "mm"), gp = gpar(fill = col["GAIN"], col = NA)) },
  LOSS = function(x, y, w, h) { grid.rect(x, y, w-unit(0.5, "mm"), h-unit(0.5, "mm"), gp = gpar(fill = col["LOSS"], col = NA)) }
)

# --- 6. ANOTACIONES Y GRÁFICO FINAL ---
# (Cálculo de Ploidy y anotaciones como ya lo tenías)
niveles_ploidia <- levels(final_matrix_extended$Ploidy)
colores_ploidia <- setNames(c("#B2DF8A", "#FDBF6F", "#A6CEE3", "#FB9A99"), niveles_ploidia)

anno_inferior = HeatmapAnnotation(
    Ploidia = final_matrix_extended$Ploidy,
    Total_Chr = anno_barplot(final_matrix_extended$TotalChr_Stimated),
    col = list(Ploidia = colores_ploidia)
)

# Dibujar OncoPrint
oncoPrint(matriz_plot,
          alter_fun = alter_fun, 
          col = col,
          bottom_annotation = anno_inferior,
          alter_fun_is_vectorized = FALSE,
          column_title = "OncoPrint OGM Detallado",
          heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Tipo de Alteración", 
                                      at = names(col), 
                                      labels = c("Alterado", "Deleción", "Duplicación", 
                                               "Translocación", "Inserción", "Ganancia", "Pérdida")))
```
